title: Covariate Shift Adaptation by Importance Weighted Cross Validation
summary: Introduces IWCV, proving unbiased model selection under covariate shift and making IWLS workflows tunable without target labels.
contributions: Empirical validation in simulation and BCI
claims: Ordinary CV is biased under covariate shift
Importance-weighted CV yields unbiased risk estimates
conclusions: IWCV is a practical selection rule for importance-weighted learning

title: Direct Importance Estimation with Model Selection and Its Application to Covariate Shift Adaptation
summary: Proposes KLIEP to estimate density ratios directly via KL minimization, avoiding separate density estimation and improving high-dimensional adaptation.
assumptions: Positive support overlap p_s(x)>0 where p_t(x)>0
contributions: Likelihood-style model selection for ratio model
claims: Direct ratio estimation is superior to density-ratio-via-density-estimation in many settings
conclusions: KLIEP improves covariate-shift correction quality
limitations: Performance degrades under severe support mismatch

title: Correcting Sample Selection Bias by Unlabeled Data
summary: Introduces Kernel Mean Matching to match source and target means in RKHS, producing instance weights for shift correction.
contributions: KMM quadratic program for importance weighting
claims: Moment matching in RKHS approximates ratio weighting
conclusions: KMM is effective for sample selection bias correction
limitations: Sensitive to kernel choice and regularization

title: A Kernel Two-Sample Test
summary: Defines MMD and hypothesis tests for distribution mismatch, widely used as a source-target discrepancy diagnostic in UDA.
claims: MMD supports nonparametric two-sample testing
conclusions: MMD is practical and statistically grounded

title: A Theory of Learning from Different Domains
summary: Provides foundational domain adaptation generalization bounds connecting target error to source error, divergence, and joint optimal hypothesis error.
claims: Domain discrepancy and source risk jointly control target risk
conclusions: Adaptation is possible when divergence and joint error are small
limitations: Bounds can be loose in deep settings

title: Domain Adaptation: Learning Bounds and Algorithms
summary: Introduces discrepancy distance-based adaptation bounds and algorithms relevant to regression and squared loss settings.
claims: Discrepancy is a better task-aware distance than generic divergences
conclusions: Minimizing empirical discrepancy improves adaptation

title: Multiple Source Adaptation and the Renyi Divergence
summary: Develops multi-source adaptation theory and weighting rules linked to Renyi divergence, directly relevant for source dataset selection among many candidates.
assumptions: Target distribution as mixture-related to sources
claims: Distribution-weighted combinations outperform naive pooling
conclusions: Careful source weighting is essential in multi-source transfer
limitations: Theory may not capture deep model optimization effects

title: Domain-Adversarial Training of Neural Networks
summary: Canonical DANN objective for learning domain-invariant features using gradient reversal; baseline for UDA comparisons.
contributions: Gradient reversal layer for adversarial adaptation
claims: Adversarial invariance improves target accuracy
conclusions: DANN is a strong general-purpose UDA baseline

title: Conditional Adversarial Domain Adaptation
summary: Extends adversarial adaptation with classifier-conditional alignment to mitigate multimodal mismatch.
assumptions: Target classes correspond to source classes
claims: Conditioning on predictions improves class-wise transfer
conclusions: CDAN outperforms vanilla adversarial alignment
future_work: Robust conditional alignment under label shift

title: Deep CORAL: Correlation Alignment for Deep Domain Adaptation
summary: Matches second-order feature statistics between domains with a simple differentiable penalty.
assumptions: Covariance alignment approximates domain discrepancy reduction
claims: Second-order alignment improves transfer with low overhead
conclusions: Deep CORAL is a strong lightweight baseline

title: Learning Transferable Features with Deep Adaptation Networks
summary: Introduces deep adaptation networks with MK-MMD penalties across layers to reduce shift.
contributions: Multi-layer MK-MMD adaptation in deep nets
claims: Joint feature learning and MMD alignment improves target risk
conclusions: DAN became a standard discrepancy baseline

title: Adversarial Discriminative Domain Adaptation
summary: Separates source pretraining and target adversarial alignment for discriminative adaptation.
claims: Discriminative adversarial adaptation can match generative methods
conclusions: Simple staged adversarial adaptation is effective

title: Maximum Classifier Discrepancy for Unsupervised Domain Adaptation
summary: Uses disagreement between task classifiers as target discrepancy signal for adaptation.
claims: Classifier discrepancy helps avoid mode collapse in alignment
conclusions: MCD improves transfer on challenging shifts

title: Bridging Theory and Algorithm for Domain Adaptation
summary: Proposes Margin Disparity Discrepancy (MDD) with generalization theory and practical algorithm.
claims: MDD yields tighter and more effective adaptation objective
conclusions: Theory-aligned discrepancy improves UDA

title: Calibrated Prediction with Covariate Shift via Unsupervised Domain Adaptation
summary: Combines importance weighting and feature adaptation for uncertainty calibration under shift, relevant to risk-aware source selection.
claims: Joint adaptation and weighting improves calibration
conclusions: Shift-aware calibration outperforms naive calibration

title: Deep Nonparametric Quantile Regression under Covariate Shift
summary: Analyzes reweighted deep nonparametric estimators under covariate shift with non-asymptotic error bounds.
claims: Reweighting improves quantile estimation under shift
conclusions: Theory supports practical reweighted deep estimators

title: When is Importance Weighting Correction Needed for Covariate Shift Adaptation?
venue: When is Importance Weighting Correction Needed for Covariate Shift Adaptation? arXiv:2303.04020.
summary: Clarifies regimes where IW is necessary, especially nonparametric misspecified settings; directly informs when IWLS-based source selection is justified.
contributions: Regime analysis for necessity of IW
claims: IW can be essential under misspecification even for nonparametric models
conclusions: Need-for-IW depends on specification and model class
future_work: Extension to deep learners and finite-sample diagnostics

title: General regularization in covariate shift adaptation
summary: Studies regularization structure under covariate shift and its interaction with importance-weighted objectives.
assumptions: Covariate shift with regularized risk minimization
claims: Regularization interacts with shift correction in nontrivial ways
conclusions: Shift-aware regularization design matters

title: ADATIME: A Benchmarking Suite for Domain Adaptation on Time Series Data
summary: Provides standardized TS-UDA evaluation without target-label leakage for model selection, central for the user’s Setting C.
assumptions: Unsupervised DA protocol should avoid target labels
claims: Evaluation inconsistencies strongly affect reported TS-UDA results
conclusions: Standardization changes method rankings and improves rigor
future_work: Expand to regression and source-selection tasks

title: Domain Adaptation for Time Series Under Feature and Label Shifts
summary: Introduces Raincoat for time-series UDA with both feature and label-shift handling.
assumptions: Complex temporal and frequency shifts across domains
claims: Joint handling of both shifts improves target performance
conclusions: Raincoat improves robustness on TS transfer tasks

title: Deep Unsupervised Domain Adaptation for Time Series Classification: a Benchmark
summary: Adds benchmark datasets/backbones for TS-UDA, useful to stress-test source-selection strategies.
contributions: Seven TS-UDA benchmark datasets and protocols
claims: Current TS-UDA methods vary strongly across shifts
conclusions: Benchmarking is prerequisite for fair progress

title: CauDiTS: Causal Disentangled Domain Adaptation of Multivariate Time Series
summary: Proposes causal-rationale disentanglement to improve robustness of TS-UDA under spurious correlations.
claims: Causal rationale extraction improves transfer reliability
conclusions: Causal priors can reduce negative transfer
limitations: Causal assumptions may not always hold

title: CALDA: Improving Multi-Source Time Series Domain Adaptation With Contrastive Adversarial Learning
summary: MS-UDA method for time series that uses cross-source contrastive structure and adversarial alignment.
assumptions: Multiple labeled sources and unlabeled target
claims: Cross-source label structure improves adaptation
conclusions: Multi-source contrastive signals are beneficial

title: Source-free time series domain adaptation with wavelet-based multi-scale temporal imputation
summary: Recent source-free TSDA method using wavelet multiscale decomposition; informative for no-source-data regimes.
assumptions: Source model available but source data unavailable
claims: Full-spectrum temporal-frequency modeling improves SFDA
conclusions: Source-free adaptation can be competitive
limitations: Not directly a source dataset selection method

title: WILDS: A Benchmark of in-the-Wild Distribution Shifts
summary: Benchmark suite for realistic distribution shifts with standardized evaluation and code.
assumptions: Real-world shifts differ from synthetic benchmarks
claims: Existing methods leave substantial OOD gaps
conclusions: Need stronger robust adaptation methods
future_work: Label-free and weakly supervised shift handling

title: Importance-weighted conditional adversarial network for unsupervised domain adaptation
summary: Combines adversarial adaptation with sample weighting and pseudo-label selection.
assumptions: Importance criteria can downweight hard-to-transfer samples
claims: Weighted adversarial training improves UDA
conclusions: Instance weighting can mitigate negative transfer

title: Importance Weighted Import Vector Machine for Unsupervised Domain Adaptation
summary: Sparse probabilistic classifier with importance-weighted model selection for UDA.
assumptions: Covariate shift and sparse kernel classifier
claims: Reliable IWCV improves parameter search
conclusions: Sparse weighted adaptation is effective

title: Multi-Source Domain Adaptation with Mixture of Experts
summary: Learns target-dependent source weighting in multi-source adaptation, conceptually aligned with dataset selection.
assumptions: Target examples can be related to sources via point-to-set metrics
claims: Adaptive source weighting beats equal pooling
conclusions: Instance-wise source relevance helps avoid negative transfer

title: Multi-Source Attention for Unsupervised Domain Adaptation
summary: Attention-based source selection over multiple domains for UDA.
claims: Attention-based source selection improves robustness
conclusions: Explainable source relevance is possible

title: ADATIME repository
summary: Reference implementation and benchmark harness for TS-UDA methods and datasets.
claims: Standardized training/evaluation pipelines
conclusions: Useful reproducibility backbone for Setting C

title: UDA-4-TSC repository
summary: Code for the deep TS-UDA benchmark, useful for rapid baseline reproduction.
claims: Supports fair comparisons
conclusions: Good candidate for reproducible experiments

title: WILDS repository
summary: Open-source benchmark package for diverse real-world distribution shifts.
claims: Improves comparability of OOD methods
conclusions: Useful for external validation of shift metrics
limitations: Limited tabular regression tasks aligned with IWLS

title: UCR Time Series Classification Archive
summary: Large library of time-series datasets; useful source pool for synthetic-to-real source selection studies.
claims: Broad benchmark coverage
conclusions: Useful source candidate bank

title: UCI HAR Dataset
summary: Widely used wearable-sensor dataset with multiple subject domains for adaptation studies.
claims: Supports domain-shift evaluations
conclusions: Common TS-UDA benchmark domain

title: WISDM dataset
summary: Smartphone/sensor activity dataset with user/domain shift variations used in TS adaptation.
claims: Useful for domain-shift experiments
conclusions: Candidate source pool for TS adaptation

title: Sleep-EDF Expanded Database
summary: PhysioNet sleep dataset often used for domain adaptation across subjects/devices.
claims: Supports cross-subject shift experiments
conclusions: Relevant for healthcare TS adaptation
limitations: Task labels not regression-oriented by default

title: Heterogeneity Activity Recognition Data Set (HHAR)
venue: Smartphone sensor data set for activity recognition.
summary: Cross-device/cross-user activity dataset useful for domain-shift experiments.
claims: Contains realistic domain shifts
conclusions: Useful in multi-source adaptation settings

title: WILDS Benchmark Website
summary: Central access point for benchmark documentation, splits, and leaderboards.
claims: Tracks in-the-wild distribution-shift performance
conclusions: Good external benchmark resource
limitations: Not specialized for IWLS source selection

title: Density Ratio Estimation in Machine Learning
summary: Comprehensive treatment of density-ratio estimation methods and theory; foundational for IWLS implementations.
assumptions: Ratio estimation as primitive for many shift problems
contributions: Unified theory and algorithms for ratio estimation
claims: Density-ratio estimation subsumes many adaptation tasks
conclusions: Direct ratio methods are central for covariate shift

title: A Review of Two Decades of Covariate Shift Adaptation
summary: Survey organizing covariate-shift correction families and practical caveats; useful for gap analysis in proposed objective design.
claims: Method behavior depends strongly on support overlap and estimation error
conclusions: Need for robust diagnostics and evaluation

title: Domain adaptation under target and conditional shift
summary: Analyzes adaptation with generalized label/conditional shift, relevant when pure covariate-shift assumptions fail in source selection.
contributions: Conditional distribution matching objective and bounds
claims: Naive invariant representations may harm under label shift
conclusions: Shift type identification is essential

title: POND: Multi Source Time Series Domain Adaptation
summary: Recent KDD work on multi-source TSDA; directly relevant to source-mixture construction in Setting B.
assumptions: Multiple source domains with target unlabeled data
claims: Improves over prior multi-source TSDA baselines
conclusions: Multi-source alignment is promising for TS

title: On Pitfalls of Test-Time Adaptation
summary: Benchmark evidence that adaptation claims are sensitive to protocol and model selection; directly relevant to no-target-label constraint.
claims: Hyperparameter/model selection dominates many reported gains
conclusions: Rigorous protocol is mandatory
limitations: TTA focus rather than source selection

title: AdaMatch: A Unified Approach to Semi-Supervised Learning and Domain Adaptation
summary: Strong modern DA baseline useful as comparator in benchmark settings.
assumptions: Consistency and confidence filtering improve adaptation
claims: Single recipe can be competitive across settings
conclusions: Useful strong baseline family

title: Importance Weighted Least Squares for Covariate Shift
venue: Importance Weighted Least Squares under Covariate Shift.
summary: Technical report focused on IWLS behavior and sample complexity under covariate shift, aligned with the user’s objective framing.
claims: Weight variance strongly affects finite-sample behavior
conclusions: Conditioning/ESS diagnostics are critical

title: Preprint arXiv:2602.02066
summary: User-provided recent related link; retrieval endpoint was unstable during scraping, so only metadata-level inclusion is provided.
limitations: Could not retrieve full text due endpoint error during this run
future_work: Retry scraping and complete structured extraction
